name: Joe Bushkin
abstract: Joseph "Joe" Bushkin (November 7, 1916 – November 3, 2004) was an American jazz pianist.

interviews.title: Joe Bushkin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

works.t: Love is Just Around the Corner

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: I made the trip back to play a couple of weeks with Joe Bushkin... Joe Bushkin again wanted one thing out of a guitar player, which was thrilling to play with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Radke
about_quotes.evidence: Well Joe was my good friend and we played together — I did his last album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Radke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Hillsdale, Michigan, November 8, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: I also during those years when I was working with Joe Bushkin, I had my own band and he was one of the members of the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: So then Joe Bushkin called me one day and says, 'I'm going into the Embers for six weeks.' I could go in with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Goodman Trio with Joe Bushkin and the drummer, Louie Bellson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 23, 2003 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: on the floor sitting at his feet was Charlie Shavers, the great trumpet player and a terrific — what a great guy Charlie was. And Joe Bushkin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 9, 2013
